title: Propagation 15: Dual-rename across validation library (15 locations)
goal:
Rename `validate` to `check` and `ValidationError` to `CheckFailure` throughout the entire codebase.

This includes:
- The class definition in core.py (ValidationError -> CheckFailure)
- The decorator function in core.py (validate -> check)
- All imports across every file
- All except/raise clauses referencing ValidationError
- All isinstance checks
- All references in rules.py, composite.py, report.py, schema.py, middleware.py, utils.py, and __init__.py

The test file (tests/test_validator.py) already uses the NEW names (check, CheckFailure). All tests must pass after the rename.

Use grep or search to locate ALL occurrences before editing. There are approximately 15 distinct locations across 8 source files that need updating.
hints: The test file already imports the new names. Focus on the 8 source files under validator/. Don't forget isinstance checks in report.py and docstring references in utils.py.

seed_files.validator/__init__.py:
from .core import validate, ValidationError
from .rules import required, min_length, max_length, matches_pattern
from .composite import all_of, any_of
from .report import ValidationReport

ground_truth: Must update 8 source files under validator/: core.py (class def + function def + all internal refs), __init__.py (imports), rules.py (import + 4 raise sites), composite.py (import + 3 except/raise sites), report.py (import + isinstance + 2 raise sites + docstring), schema.py (import + except + docstring), middleware.py (import + except), utils.py (import + except + docstrings). Tests already use new names and will pass once all 15 locations are updated.

eval_spec.check_script_content:
#!/bin/bash
cd "$WORKDIR"
set +e
SCORE=0
TOTAL=10

# Check 1: Tests pass
OUTPUT=$(python3 -m pytest tests/test_validator.py -v 2>&1)
PASSED=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -c " PASSED")
TOTAL_TESTS=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -cE " (PASSED|FAILED)")
if [ "$PASSED" -eq "$TOTAL_TESTS" ] && [ "$TOTAL_TESTS" -gt 0 ]; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: All tests pass ($PASSED)"
else
    echo "  FAIL: $PASSED/$TOTAL_TESTS tests pass"
    echo "$OUTPUT" | grep "FAILED" | head -5
fi

# Check 2: No ValidationError in source files
COUNT=$(grep -r "ValidationError" validator/ --include="*.py" | grep -v __pycache__ | wc -l)
if [ "$COUNT" -eq 0 ]; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: No ValidationError references"
else
    echo "  FAIL: $COUNT ValidationError references remain"
fi

# Check 3: No 'validate' function/decorator in source files (but allow validate_data etc.)
COUNT=$(grep -rP "(?<![_a-zA-Z])validate(?![_a-zA-Z])" validator/ --include="*.py" | grep -v __pycache__ | grep -v validate_data | grep -v validate_or_raise | grep -v is_valid | wc -l)
if [ "$COUNT" -eq 0 ]; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: No bare 'validate' references"
else
    echo "  FAIL: $COUNT bare 'validate' references remain"
fi

# Check 4: CheckFailure class exists in core.py
if grep -q "class CheckFailure" validator/core.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: core.py has CheckFailure class"
else
    echo "  FAIL: core.py missing CheckFailure"
fi

# Check 5: check function exists in core.py
if grep -q "def check" validator/core.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: core.py has check function"
else
    echo "  FAIL: core.py missing check function"
fi

# Check 6: __init__.py imports correct names
if grep -q "CheckFailure" validator/__init__.py && grep -q "check" validator/__init__.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: __init__.py imports check and CheckFailure"
else
    echo "  FAIL: __init__.py missing correct imports"
fi

# Check 7: rules.py uses CheckFailure
if grep -q "CheckFailure" validator/rules.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: rules.py uses CheckFailure"
else
    echo "  FAIL: rules.py missing CheckFailure"
fi

# Check 8: composite.py uses CheckFailure
if grep -q "CheckFailure" validator/composite.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: composite.py uses CheckFailure"
else
    echo "  FAIL: composite.py missing CheckFailure"
fi

# Check 9: report.py uses CheckFailure
if grep -q "CheckFailure" validator/report.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: report.py uses CheckFailure"
else
    echo "  FAIL: report.py missing CheckFailure"
fi

# Check 10: utils.py uses check (not validate)
if grep -q "is_validated\|@check\|_is_validated" validator/utils.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: utils.py references check correctly"
else
    echo "  FAIL: utils.py missing check references"
fi

echo ""
echo "Score: $SCORE/$TOTAL"

if [ "$SCORE" -ge 10 ]; then
    exit 0
else
    exit 1
fi
